programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: The dramatic story of the twin sisters defrauded by a sinister con artist masquerading as a generous, fun-loving guy who wasn't such a hero after all.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host the battle of numbers, letters and laughs. Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang play Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. With David O'Doherty.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Emma and James look into creating a not-for-profit visitor attraction with all the rescue animals they've taken in.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Sidhbh and Neasa launch their new Miami clinic with a party in an operating theatre. And Orlando wrestler D-Wayne contends with a bad knee.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, can the Rodriguezes' lucky sale streak continue for Steve and Amanda? And in Murcia, the Browns hunt for a family-friendly holiday home for Steve and Hannah.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Belinda and Alan from Essex have been dreaming of a holiday home on Spain's Costa del Sol for years, and have now decided to buy around the pretty seaside resort of La Cala de Mijas.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: On Spain's Orihuela Costa, Can Jean Johansson find a holiday home that works for all three generations of Karen and Paul's family, for £75,000?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Annette and Mark dream of finding the perfect holiday home with a pool on Spain's Orihuela Costa for £150k. Jasmine Harman's on hand to make sure the search goes swimmingly.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In Brittany, Donna and Paul need a winch to complete their huge balcony installation. At Château de Pican, Jane and Rory have a literally smashing time in their gîte kitchen.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back on one of their most turbulent builds ever: Amber and Martin's working from home transformation in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The week's competition is in and around Belfast, where first to host is chatty PR manager Claire. She aims to impress with a menu made up of her favourite restaurant dishes, but never having plucked a pheasant before she has a challenge on her hands.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second day in and around Belfast, it's the turn of carpenter and country boy Neil to do his best to land this week's £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Third host from Belfast is 57-year-old window cleaning business owner and keen golfer Paul. Debonair Paul has cut a dash already this week with his sense of style and now he's hoping to impress with his fine food.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hosting in and around Belfast is livewire of the group, 28-year-old DJ and events organiser Ryan. With the bar set high in the competition, the pressure is on for the man who has hosted events for thousands.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night of competition in Belfast and host Cailin is pinning her hopes of winning on a Eurovision extravaganza.

programme.title: Cruising to the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: In Alaska, two Royal Princess crewmates pull on their lumberjack boots in the forest around Ketchikan, while an adventurous couple tackle Skagway's rocks and mountain trails.

programme.title: Jamie's Ultimate BBQ
programme.desc: Jamie turns his outdoor grill skills to a butterflied leg of lamb with creamy beans and leeks, and grilled fish tacos with a charred fruit salsa to warm up any weeknight.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britain's favourite personalities hit the sofa as they watch the week's big shows, sharing their sharp, insightful and funny points of view.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Will former mayor Tracey take the plunge with toilet roll salesman Stuart? And can burlesque dancer Double D and professional singer Lee come together and hit all the right notes?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 11-year-old is brought in after being struck by a car. A woman has tripped over her cat. And a doctor describes the painful decision to live away from home for the pandemic.

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: A patient with throat cancer needs a new windpipe to save his voice. A rare cancer means Melissa's entire reproductive system must be removed for her to stay alive.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The crew of Warwickshire, Northamptonshire, Derbyshire, Leicestershire and Rutland Air Ambulance race to reach Penny, who is in anaphylactic shock after a wasp sting.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: It's the Playtime phase as the virgins are encouraged to explore their sexual fantasies and embrace their kinky side - from submission and dominance to bondage and impact play.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The group explore basics and intimacy. They practice kissing. Ellen gets naked. A kink session unlocks Millie's desires. An islander comes out. And another loses their virginity.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Ant and Mark's dogged pursuit of a stolen moped takes them off their patch into the estates of Sheffield.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Liam and police dog Vader bring down a fleeing suspect, Kev and Spike rescue a man trapped in an overturned car, Pete is forced to pull his taser, and Spike disguises himself as a pizza delivery boy to carry out an unconventional raid.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Kev and Pete pursue a runaway car into a pitch-black farmer's field, Jacko takes part in a major heroin bust, and rookie police dog Elsa chases a runaway burglar through the streets of Middlesbrough.

programme.title: Belle Starr
programme.desc: Belle Starr is the daughter of a Southern aristocrat. When the invading Yankees take over her land, she vows revenge and heads to Missouri where she marries Confederate outlaw Sam Starr, and joins him and his gang in harrying the occupying troops. In fact, the real Belle Starr was a simple outlaw who, with her Cherokee husband, used the opportunity of the confusion of war to line their own pockets.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In a quiet rural village in Leicestershire, a utility truck has got stuck in the mud in a local resident's garden. Adam and his team are under pressure to get it out without causing thousands of pounds worth of damage to the property.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Jim calls for back up when an angry, uninsured driver is egged on by a hostile mob.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A dangerous drug dealer lets far more than the cat out of the bag, name swapping friends leave the interceptors baffled, and Phil has a shock for an illegal driver while Mac deals with a drunk drummer.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Concerned 999 callers report an HGV swerving all over the M6. PCs Jamie Barlow and Amy Hollis team up to search for the vehicle in question. When they finally catch up with the driver, they discover the awful truth behind his dangerous driving.

programme.title: Highway Thru Hell
programme.desc: The closures of two major highways cause drivers to voice their frustration which leaves Gazzola in a sour mood; Ken arrives at a job to find that the competition has gotten the jump on him.

programme.title: Motorway: Hell on the Highway
programme.desc: How junctions can cause driving disasters, the dangers of joining a motorway, and a multi-car crash caused by a group of cars racing on a highway in New York at night time.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Not knowing whom they can trust, a reunited Mulder and Scully must continue their investigation without the help of the FBI.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: One of the toughest and most notorious prisons in Ukraine. It is said that no one has ever managed to escape. Over eight hundred prisoners are behind bars in Colony 8 under maximum security.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Robbie's house moving crew faces a difficult job transporting a large house in dangerous, wet conditions. Also, Mark is on a deadline to transport mining gear into opal country.

programme.title: Trucking Hell

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: Near the junction to East Midlands airport, a trailer sheds its load and causes rush hour chaos. Adam dispatches a crack team of his finest recovery ops, in the hope they will be able to resolve the situation quickly.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Dave is on the lookout for a car driving dangerously, when suddenly it pulls out in front of him. John's 100mph blue-light run panics an unrelated driver into a crash. Veteran cop Jason's `copper's instinct' leads him to suspect a motorcyclist.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Mac stops a driver for having no insurance and unearths a huge drugs haul and the chopper tracks down two notorious burglars. Elsewhere, Tim and Ange stop an alleged road-rage suspect armed with a table leg, and Phil wakes up the neighbours in his search for a couple of taxi-fare dodgers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A pursuit through Sheffield lands two known offenders in deep trouble, Phil pulls over a Lamborghini that is not all that it seems to be, and Mac has a close shave when he comes face to face with a gang of hoodies. Elsewhere, Ange gets the runaround from a four-legged foe.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Andy Doran is chasing a gang of thieves fleeing a shop robbery in Ellesmere Port. And PC Chris Swash races to the scene of a head-on car collision that has left a driver trapped.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A drone hunts a prolific criminal after their car crashes, a suspicious car with learner plates is pulled over, and PCs pursue a driver high on laughing gas.

programme.title: Police: Hour Of Duty
programme.desc: Fighting crime against the clock: the officers of Derbyshire Police Force tackle a range of incidents, from drug house stakeouts to missing children and domestic violence calls, all in one hectic real-life hour.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully suspect a Midwestern teenager is responsible for a series of lightning-related deaths.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: Maafushi Central Prison is a high-security prison covering an area the size of ten football pitches. Surrounded by six-meter-high walls, and the sea, the facility houses up to 800 criminals under the strictest security measures.

programme.title: Outback Truckers

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Singer Laura-Elen has a trial gig at a Fuengirola bar, determined to secure a regular slot to add to her wedding fund. Bambi reveals her rural lifestyle.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In 2017 and 2018, there were 27 fatal police-related road traffic incidents, resulting in 29 fatalities.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Matt Edwards is on the hunt for a gang of suspected teenage car thieves in Derby, thought to have been breaking into houses to steal car keys before driving off with the occupants' vehicles.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Traffic Cop Stuart's first task is to attend the scene of an accident in which a motorbike rider has been catapulted from his bike on a bend. Officers also attend the site of a horrific head on collision involving a transit van and an HGV.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Charlie's first solo pursuit is at 115mph after a dangerous driver who takes risk after risk. Sandra and Chris face a battle to bag a breath sample from a suspected drink driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: On an epic manhunt, a police dog becomes star of the show after tracking suspects a mile away. And when interceptors wake a suspected burglar, he sinks his teeth into an officer's leg.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: An alarming report of a gang wielding machetes to steal the keys to a BMW leads to a pursuit on the M1. But will the bad guys slip away after going through a gap on the hard shoulder?

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: An armed robbery abroad leaves one claimant massively out of pocket, but the police report tells a completely different story. Meanwhile, dash cam footage exposes a bogus accident. (S2 Ep11)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Cash is out for a run, and runs into Jo who is missing having Tane in the Bay.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie comes home, beaming after her recent ultrasound and the fact that she got to see her healthy baby.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the every day lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals can't.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Russell, 83, is suffering from agonising abdominal pain. Over in Paediatrics, paramedics rush in four-year-old George who - distressingly - is struggling to breathe.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Following specialist emergency response teams on their most extreme medical call outs, embedded with the Critical Care and Air Ambulance Response cars.

programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Doctor Cyril Taft experiences the rush of an NCIS case after he is assigned surgical duty to Gibb's suspect and finds key evidence. Also, Dinozzo pays Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS agents' personal plans are interrupted when each of them catches errors in a seemingly cut-and-dried closed case. Also, McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of not discussing work during dinner, while DiNozzo bores his date with shop talk.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a trio of combat vets are found murdered in a D.C. motel room, NCIS tracks down a suspect who refuses to talk to anyone but Sam Hanna.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Benson find themselves on opposite sides when Valerie Sennet accuses her estranged husband, Miles Sennet, of breaking into her home and raping her. Miles has no history of violence and Valerie refuses to allow detectives to use her daughter Tessa as a witness, he is soon released, and Stabler and Benson continue to clash as Benson remains convinced that Miles raped Valerie and is a danger.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Tutuola reluctantly works on a case involving the rape of a coed after his son, Ken feels responsible for catching the rapist. Tutuola trying to solve the case with the rape victim unwilling to cooperate crosses paths with another detective, Chester Lake, who is also investigating similar cases as part of Brooklyn SVU. The two clash, but when another rape occurs, the two detectives must work together.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When an eight-year-old propositions a male classmate, Stabler and Tutuola are called in to investigate the case and soon learn that Jake Winnock's father spent seven years in prison for molesting his older brother Teddy. DNA tests clear Gregory Hensal of abusing his son, but implicate Jake's older brother, whom detectives soon learn has been running his own personal Internet pornography site.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A teenage girl makes a false rape allegation against two boys at her school, before driving her car into a crowd of bystanders in a suicide attempt. programme.desc: The first round of the quiz competition for students continues with Oriel College, Oxford playing Christ's College, Cambridge for a place in the second round. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: The brains of Britain pit their wits against Victoria Coren Mitchell and her puzzling questions. The County Councillors take on the Science Writers in this first quarter-final.

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin, Freida Pinto and Jon Gupta reveal the hidden wonders of India's surprising natural world. This is a land where the tea comes with added elephants, gibbons sing to greet the morning, tigers dance and lions roam.

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Denmark
programme.desc: In episode two of Andrew Graham-Dixon's epic journey through Scandinavian art and landscape, Denmark emerges from modest beginnings to become one of the greatest powers and arbiters of taste in northern Europe, a story of incredible transformation befitting the homeland of the greatest fairytale spinner of them all, Hans Christian Anderson, creator of The Ugly Duckling and The Emperor's New Clothes.

programme.title: Gluck: Who Did She Think He Was?
programme.desc: The untold story of Britain's cross-dressing high society painter. Gluck was one of the British Establishment's go-to portrait painters of the 1930s. Her shows were attended by royalty, aristocrats and celebrities.

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Bordering the Caspian Sea, Baku offers a tantalising mix of the ancient and modern - at the crossroads of east meets west, on the ancient silk trading route. It is also an authoritarian state, where cultural life is tightly controlled.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The world wars force artists to grapple with destruction and savagery, challenging conventions and the old imperial order, while those fleeing war bring new ideas to Britain.

programme.title: Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: A tribute to Dame Margot Fonteyn, first broadcast shortly after her death in 1991. Featuring interviews with colleagues and friends and extracts from the ballets she performed in throughout her career.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Is a house in Wales the smallest Martin has ever encountered in his 20 years on the show? Meanwhile, Dion is captivated by a rather funky, retro carpet in a terrace in Lancashire. And Tommy's in Wandsworth, London, where a one-bedroom garden flat has potential to add space.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams head to an antiques fair in Builth Wells in Mid Wales. programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Adam and Lucy race against the clock to produce milk by their contract deadline.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie is in the Cairngorms to see how precious habitats are being protected against wildfires. Meanwhile, Anne takes a trip to the Isle of May to uncover her family's lighthouse history.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: It's time for some early summer colour as Carole Baxter is at Glenarn in the first of her visits to classic Scottish gardens.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Amy Canavan presents highlights from the weekend's fixtures in the SWPL.

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: Today, trainee harbour pilot Ralph Greig faces a tough test on the simulator on his first day of training. Meanwhile, ex-sailor Ellen Haughland has her heart set on stepping into Ralph's shoes as a VTS controller - can she become Aberdeen's first ever woman in that role? Onboard the Bibby Sapphire, the crew brace themselves for a force ten storm heading directly towards them.

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: In this edition, fish merchant Doug Rennie contends with a move to a new state-of-the-art fish processing factory. Plus, the Highland Prince loads up with vital supplies for a North Sea oil rig, new pilot Ralph Greig's training illustrates why he has one of the most dangerous jobs in the harbour, and the harbour chaplain's tireless work to open a sea farer's centre in Aberdeen finally pays off.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: With Ken needing to re-sit his driving exam in order to win back his licence, Richard offers to take him out on a mock test, not realising just how rusty his dad has become behind the wheel.

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: After an emotional funeral for his dad, Colin and Cathy have the neighbours round but understandably Colin is not his usual upbeat self. Christine was not impressed with the service or the priest and, as usual, is not backward about coming forward with her opinions on the whole affair. And as Beth, Eric and Ian try to make their excuses to leave early, Cathy manages to foil their escape by inviting more of the neighbours in.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Stevens & McCarthy
programme.desc: At the roll van, Davie is not happy with the chat, a gamer mum loses her cool, and the girls think twinkly lights are the answer to all life's woes.

programme.title: Banjo and Ro's Grand Island Hotel
programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo and husband Ro arrive on Ulva to revive a derelict mansion, battling a tiny budget, island logistics and their first big test - the crumbling library.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Fufe, it's a critical year for Andy and Jo, who have taken out a £2 million loan to get into arable farming. In Dumfries and Galloway, Nick and Holly are preparing to take over the family farm and experimenting with a new breed of sheep.

programme.title: Janey
programme.desc: Janey Godley is one of Britain's leading comedians. This film follows Janey on her 2023 Not Dead Yet tour, following her diagnosis of terminal cancer. Janey is touring with her best mate Shirley and her daughter, comedian Ashley Storrie.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: The Secret Lives of Lighthouse Keepers
programme.desc: A glimpse into the working world of the lighthouse keepers, technicians, seafarers, and designers who are responsible for keeping the lights on in 208 lighthouses round the coast of Scotland and the Isle of Man.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: T in the Park
programme.desc: Edith Bowman delves deep into the BBC archive to revisit stellar performances from an array of artists, including Oasis, Emeli Sande and Blur.

programme.title: TUNE
programme.desc: Dundee star Hannah Laing's career has taken her across the globe. As she prepares to headline a historic homecoming show at the Caird Hall, she reveals how she switched from dental nurse to disc jockey.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve is in Australia with the world's most venomous fish and a bat-eating snake.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall searches for meerkats, aardwolves and aardvarks.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: This time, Stacey and her team - organizer Dilly, carpenter Rob and cleaner Iwan - help the Bradshaw family part with 30 years of clutter in just seven days. It's the biggest challenge the team have faced so far, but it could help the Bradshaws fall back in love with their stunningly beautiful home. Retired electrician Wayne is a car boot sale enthusiast, and daughter Harriet worries that all the clutter is too much for her parents. Craft-loving Sue emotionally reveals that her son Alex is too embarrassed to bring people home. Stacey gathers up all their possessions, including 100 knitting needles, 81 fondue forks and 204 lipsticks, and lays them out for all to see. Sue and Wayne struggle to let go of over 30 years' worth of stuff, and tensions rise as Harriet and Alex try to help their parents make important decisions. At the house, carpenter Rob breathes new life into the home by rejigging the kitchen layout and upcycling old but much-loved items.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: After being invited to a bar crawl on Khaosan Road, Zara gets under the skin of Bangkok's notorious red light district, Soi Cowboy - but filming is swiftly shut down by local bar owners and the police. When Zara meets Mike Yu, a young British expat living a life of luxury in the Thai capital, she realises there's more to the city than meets the eye. Meanwhile, a visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law in Thailand.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: In Asia's sex capital, Pattaya, Zara meets British men chasing cheap thrills, before heading to Ko Phangan, an exotic island famed for its spirituality and beauty.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: After a wild and crazy night at Ko Phangan's Full Moon Party, Zara travels to Phuket and experiences a taste of Thailand's luxury lifestyle.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception - conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The BBC's big comedy talent search, the New Comedy Awards is scouring the country to uncover the hottest new stand-ups. Ee head to the capital and the historic Alexandra Palace, home of the BBC's first broadcast. Five comics will battle it out to book their place in this year's grand final showdown, where one will be crowned the BBC New Comedian of 2025. Hosting the comedy extravaganza is comedian, actor, presenter and writer Sara Pascoe.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Finally back together, Conor and Jock meet an older couple who seem similar in many ways, then accidentally take them hostage.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide to expand their empire in Cork and take new kids on the block, Cormac and Jack, under their wing.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Linda Walsh, Conor's one true love, is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure through Uzbekistan reaches its finale in the historic city of Bukhara. He visits a centuries-old Jewish community and experiences the life of nomadic tribes.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Ireland's greatest garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Brighton, Zoe Ball wants to help retired woodworker Neville get his health back on track after a series of heart attacks. Her prescription is delightfully simple: add a little sunshine into his life.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights from seven games, including Newcastle against West Ham, with Nuno Espírito Santo's side desperate for points in the relegation battle. Aston Villa take on Liverpool, Nottingham Forest go to Manchester United, and there's plenty to play for in the race for the final few European spots as Brentford host Crystal Palace, Brighton make the trip to Leeds, Everton face Sunderland, and Wolves welcome Fulham to Molineux.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions, which are on Offa's Dyke Path, the life and works of John Cage, the wines of Portugal and Only Fools and Horses.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds, testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst the ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants' motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you'll be all right!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team visit north London to help solve a family of four's housing woes. In one day, and on a family friendly budget, they have to create a home office for a working mum, deep clean the appliances and find space for the family's plant collection.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox embarks upon an exploration of how a few fundamental laws gave birth to the most complex, diverse and unique feature of our universe - life. In this episode, he journeys to the volcanic landscapes of South-East Asia, seeking to understand how life first began and how that spark has endured to this day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team travel to St Austell in Cornwall where they encounter a sock-making machine and two stuffed eagles, personal memorabilia from a member of the 1932 Olympic swimming team, a gold watch given for a heroic exploit and a lifetime's collection of greetings cards.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from Oxford Union, right in the heart of Oxford. Paul Martin is joined by experts Christina Trevanion and Will Axon. Together the team pick out a selection of interesting antiques and collectables to be sold at a local auction.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a stunning railway adventure through the western Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica, beginning in Sardinia's capital, Cagliari.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores some of this year's most exciting archaeological finds from the north of Britain. Each discovery comes straight from the site, filmed by the archaeologists themselves. Alice discovers the well-preserved writing tablets, swords and domestic items left by Romans at Vindolanda during a time of British rebellion.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: The annual Royal Horticultural Society Chelsea Flower Show is considered the most famous flower show in the United Kingdom, attracting visitors from all over the world.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates the turning points that led to America's declaration of independence from Britain in 1776 and asks whether this explosive split could have been avoided.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob head to the beautiful River Ouse in the heart of Bedfordshire. The river was once famous for barbel, but has recently become renowned for its big perch instead, a fish that has so far eluded them. After a long day fishing under the summer sun on the Turvey estate, Bob prepares a dinner that Paul will never forget - slow-cooked tripe.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: When the bomb detonates inside Harvey's Wagon Wheel Casino, it leaves behind more than shattered concrete and twisted steel: a maze of unanswered questions, until a lucky break leads to a psychological stand-off.

programme.title: Destroyer
programme.desc: As a young cop, Erin Bell went under cover to infiltrate a gang in the California desert -- with tragic results. When the gang leader re-emerges, Bell embarks on an obsessive quest to bring him to justice and make peace with her tortured past.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Adam Henson visit Oswestry Livestock Market, one of the region's largest weekly cattle and sheep markets. As spring trading peaks, they step into a fast-moving world where fortunes can change in minutes. With livestock numbers falling and markets closing nationwide, Oswestry remains a crucial lifeline, not just for trade, but for farming communities to come together.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: A boycott and politics threaten the opening ceremony after a visit from its creative director. Meanwhile, it's time for Ian to think about life after football.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the Georgian era in her hometown of Bristol, from architectural gems to the world's first chocolate factory, backstreet gin and the horror of the slave trade.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Edwardian Britain by visiting Cardiff, which grew rich selling the coal that powered the Empire. Alice hears the stories of the Edwardian miners and their backbreaking and often dangerous work.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his antique dealers are in Bolton where two toilets turn up for James Layte, an Action Man commands Jo Brayshaw's attention and a heart-shaped pendant arrives. Does the owner know how much it is worth though?

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys are wowed by a first-edition copy of Henry David Thoreau's classic Walden. Plus, interviews begin to find a new broker for the night shift.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys check out a 1920s doll of aviator Charles Lindbergh, as well as a harmonica that formerly belonged to Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy brings along Bogart's sisters to try and throw him off at the auction. Meanwhile Ursula is in a bad mood and the others try to take advantage.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Ursula is in need of a change of luck with her shop suffering from poor sales. Meanwhile Rick and Cindy argue about marriage.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: At storage auctions across Canada, a group of high-stakes buyers convene daily, bidding against one another on repossessed and forgotten storage units in search of profit.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The buyers look for deals in the city of Longview, Lesa and Jerry battle Jenny and Mary, Rudy seeks a strange weapon, Ricky and Bubba score more than they can handle.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: Englishman David Kay joins the auction action in Fort Worth, Victor uncovers spy gear, Ricky and Bubba try to land something large and dangerous.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: An abandoned ghost town in a remote desert with a dark past; when a strange facility is spotted in Russia, experts investigate if it's hiding evidence of alien life; cutting-edge tech reveals new clues to these sinister mysteries from space.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: When satellites spot a mysterious structure in North Korea, experts investigate if this sinister evidence points to a looming nuclear threat, and using cutting-edge tech, they reveal dark secrets from this strange place.

programme.title: Legends of the Pharaohs
programme.desc: Only the third woman to become Pharaoh in the 3,000-year history of Ancient Egypt, Hatshepsut catapulted to power 14 centuries before Cleopatra.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys check out a collection of antique cast-iron piggy banks and a rare triple-barrelled firearm. They also hunt for a new broker to cover the night shift.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Great finds come thick and fast, as the guys lay their hands on a rare photograph of Albert Einstein and a collection of top-secret documents from the Pentagon.

programme.desc: Roy decides to bring his young nephew and protege to experience the highs and lows of an auction in South Etobicoke.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: One of the most prolific inventors of the 20th century, Nikola Tesla shepherded mankind to a new industrial era and laid the groundwork for today's technological age. Is it possible that he was influenced by some otherworldly design?

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Uncovering new evidence pointing to the possibility that ancient man was visited by extraterrestrials. Influencing ancient technologies, architecture and art.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The buyers look for deals in the city of Longview, Lesa and Jerry battle Jenny and Mary, Rudy seeks a strange weapon, Ricky and Bubba score more than they can handle.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: Englishman David Kay joins the auction action in Fort Worth, Victor uncovers spy gear, Ricky and Bubba try to land something large and dangerous.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy brings along Bogart's sisters to try and throw him off at the auction. Meanwhile Ursula is in a bad mood and the others try to take advantage.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Ursula is in need of a change of luck with her shop suffering from poor sales. Meanwhile Rick and Cindy argue about marriage.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers are at the Reebok Stadium in Bolton, where a Hermes travel clock gets Jo Brayshaw's heart racing. Some Bolton Wanderers memorabilia comes Simon Schneider's way, while a tiger's eye parasol grabs everyone's attention.

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: After lab tests reveal stunning clues about the ceramic material, the team faces a hard decision about their investigation of the Mesa.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In 2016, a mysterious illness strikes dozens of government officials in Cuba; hundreds more are afflicted.

programme.title: Hazardous History With Henry Winkler
programme.desc: Ever taken medical advice from a cigarette commercial? There was a time when any and all cures could be sold to the public indiscriminately. Dive into a world where animal skin could replace your own, and where doctors gave coffee enemas.

programme.title: Discovered BY Disaster
programme.desc: Inspect the wreck of the Mary Celestia, a Civil War ship raised by a hurricane. Encounter imperial relics exposed by a drought in Iraq, the storm-tossed remains of a medieval man, and long-lost wooden artefacts that reshape Native American history.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Uncovering new evidence pointing to the possibility that ancient man was visited by extraterrestrials. Influencing ancient technologies, architecture and art.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Modern Day Dinosaur Sighting, Mysterious Lights Above Norway and Girl Punches Down Tree
programme.desc: Did a Japanese film crew discover proof of a dinosaur living in the African Congo? Are reports of the strange lights above the Hessdalen Valley in Norway a sign of extraterrestrial activity?

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-Trospective
programme.desc: ...Henry's Wreck-trospective. Henry and Sam start at Bristol Classic Car Show, where they acquire a quirky car from the 1970s. (S1, ep 1)

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les works at breakneck pace to customise a $20,000 diamond ring, and when Ashley unjustifiably derails the sale, noone blames Seth for hitting the roof.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea replaces Supertato with an evil robot potato to ruin his reputation.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Pando is at Bing's house today and has an earworm called Bingly Bangly. Bing isn't impressed with this, declaring that he is Bing. programme.title: Mojo Swoptops

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Join the chickens in space for the big finale of Agony and Eggstacy.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are out walking around in the rain with Duggee and notice a rainbow. But before they know it, it's gone and the Squirrels are devastated. They decide to make their own rainbow and go off in search of all the colours from everything around them, including strawberries, carrots and bananas. Duggee shows the Squirrels what he has been working on too, a rainbow cake.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Dad's accused of fluffing in Bluey's face as he climbs out of bed, he's put on trial with Mum as the judge, and Bluey and Bingo as witnesses. Bluey might also have to explain what she was doing there.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Bluey has a play date at Chloe's, Bingo must learn how to play without her sister. However, all the games she knows need more than one person. Luckily, Dad has a problem that needs solving.

programme.desc: Glitch and his grimbots have a long race on a very long wall - the Great Wall of China.

programme.desc: When Peter makes a rash promise to appease an angry Tommy Brock, he and his friends find themselves worming for their lives.

programme.desc: A poisonous sea krait lays her eggs in the garden pod, just as Captain Barnacles has to navigate the ship past a treacherous whirlpool.

programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Alphabet song
programme.desc: Mrs Weaver raps the alphabet with Duggee and the Squirrels.

programme.desc: Bella and Lift visit the Fortress of Fun, where Kingsley and Queenie have a fun snail slime race. But who will win, Team King or Team Queen?

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Children's animation series. Bing makes Sula a picture with all her favourite things on it. It just needs one last jewel to be stuck on but the glue spills all over the picture. Sparkles save the day.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'chair', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see a brown, a comfy and a high chair, as well as a pushchair. We meet a friend who has a wheelchair and sit at the beach in some deck chairs. The journey is repeated with some surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee's friend finally succeeds in saying today's word.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: The bugs get creative with new chalk until some of it runs out.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou are invited to lunch with two very slow snails, Hemi and Lemi. Kiri is famished, but she tries to be polite and not gnash her teeth.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: Ziff, Pipp and Button use kitchen foil to create and launch an out-of-this-world spaceship.

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies go `this way, that way' on a Tubby Car journey to the Hidey Hup. The Tubby Spinny goes `this way, that way' too.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes

programme.title: I Can Grow It

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca is keen to go on a travelling adventure and invites Murphy to come. Murphy's time machine takes them to the dinosaur age, but when it stops working, they need to get making. Firstly, they create a paper plate dinosaur, followed by some colourful water blob creatures made by the Mini Monsters. Then, Rebecca gets a call on the Magic Monster Mirror from her aunt Antarctic, who makes herself some snow-building buddies to help rescue her snowman.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are having a picnic and notice that the chickens eat differently than they do. Duggee has his eating badge and can explain all the different ways animals eat. Some animals eat slowly like cows, who eat all day long. But others eat quickly like frogs. Some, like goats, are always eating, while tortoises can go a whole year without food. Some animals eat food that is very high up, like giraffes, and some eat food that is very low down, like moles.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Audrey and her family visit a farm. They meet some of the animals up close, and Audrey feeds the guinea pigs with a carrot treat before enjoying cuddles with a baby goat. Then it's off for a tractor ride, where Audrey meets a horse with a moustache!

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Jake is making traditional Australian meat pies using a recipe that his dad has shown him. Jake makes two sets of different sized pastry circles and fills them with a mixture made from lamb mince. One of Jake's ingredients is black pepper, but his friends are not too sure about the taste when they try it before the meal. Let's hope they enjoy it in the cooked pies!

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie finds out how really tall buildings are made out of steel and concrete. She visits a skyscraper construction site to explain how the concrete columns that are buried deep into the ground help to make it strong and stable. Then Maddie uses a special 360 camera to see all around her from the top floor. Next Maddie is finding out how birds fly.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Kika and her family are on a day out to the Woofton Transport Museum. But when their new audio guide starts malfunctioning, Kika needs her supersight to race back through the museum.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Zebra and Red-Bellied Piranha
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie head to a safari park to meet a herd of zebras. They reveal that a zebra's stripes are unique to each individual. Not one zebra looks the same. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie encounter some red-bellied piranhas. These fish have huge teeth, and they see a large shoal of piranhas right in front of them being fed their breakfast. Finally, Aneeshwar and Lizzie discover what the wild connection is between these two incredible animals.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's early in the morning after the Brooklyn-Bells have returned from holiday and there's someone banging on their front door. It's Mrs Hobbs and she's furious. Waffle left Mrs Hobbs' kitchen taps running and now her home is flooded and there's water pouring out of her front door.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Mama search for fresh seaweed for the nest, while Papa and Baba guard the burrow from the seagulls. Oona finds that Mama loves to have fun and is very, very brave.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The potatoes go on an imaginary hippy journey and sing about the amazing things you can do when you give imagination a try.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: TapTap's sister, TipTop, comes for a visit and amazes Yukee with her drumming skills. She is deaf, and she drums just as well as TapTap, making Yukee wonder how she does it. With TipTop's help, Yukee learns how to feel the music.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: While watching TV, Maddie and Triggs keep annoying each other with their noisy snacking! But luckily Dad is on hand to help them remember the things they like about each other.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Roly makes his usual noise in the Clubhouse, Duggee and the Squirrels reminisce about how completely different Roly was when he first arrived. Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasn't loud at all but impossibly quiet. As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks present their very own, very numbery version of the classic tale.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock I sings a song. Ben the Pen makes the words appear so that we can read along.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends are at a racetrack, where they have lots of fun with different types of bicycles. Meanwhile, the Tumbles are getting ready for the Great Golden Egg Race.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's time to wake up, but Tinky Winky isn't feeling well. Soon after, Tinky Winky feels better, but now Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po don't feel well.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how butterflies and moths play an important role in the environment. They find out that they are fantastic pollinators who help to create habitats for other creatures to live. They learn how their poo can return nutrients to the soil and how butterflies and moths can be food for other animals too. On the way, they get up close to a dragonfly and turn into Rambler butterflies!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: It's Sophie's first day. She worries that she's no good at football, but feels like part of the team when she helps the others work together to create a new football kit.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all very little. Grace uses a little pick up truck to take some hay to the horses, shows off a little plane that can only take one person and then goes for a ride on a mini motorbike.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: On show and tell day, Bertie shares with the class why he loves gardening.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Making it on time for Movie Night proves to be an incredible adventure.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Paani finds a lonely, worried frog, and with the help of the Octonauts, searches for his family.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Locked Cage
programme.desc: When Mr McGregor locks him in a cage with Sammy Whiskers, Peter must join forces with the rat to escape.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Happy has always loved the water, even though it makes a mess. But while swimming around in his paddling pool at the playhouse, Duggee reminds the Squirrels that there was a time when Happy feared water. On Happy's first day, his mum drops him off and he is excited. He goes to play with Norrie and Roly and has loads of fun baking, playing with toys and jumping over puddles, but Happy can't do it.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Vida helps Tidbit when his new pet plant, Planthony, isn't very well.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato's dancing is too powerful and destructive, but when he has to face Evil Pea in a dance-off to save his trapped friends, he soon realises his moves can save the day.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston left an egg sandwich out for too long, and now its explosive smell is stinking up the whole town. Mojo and Bo need to evacuate everyone in Swoppiton before the eggy stinkplosion!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Dad's accused of fluffing in Bluey's face as he climbs out of bed, he's put on trial with Mum as the judge, and Bluey and Bingo as witnesses. Bluey might also have to explain what she was doing there.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Bluey has a play date at Chloe's, Bingo must learn how to play without her sister. However, all the games she knows need more than one person. Luckily, Dad has a problem that needs solving.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Zog cures Fifi's hiccups with a big dragon roar. When patients are too scared to be treated by him, Zog has to choose between being a scary dragon or a doctor.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum is determined to perform Great-Granny Plum's song at the Balamory ceilidh all by himself, but he soon realises it isn't easy being a one-man band.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle discovers he has a talent for painting Mrs Hobbs's cat, George, and decides to share his work with her as a way to get in her good books.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi freezes the café to read, but frozen chaos leads her home to a cosy reading corner.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: It's Time to Look at Old Pictures
programme.desc: A photo of Gran Gran and JoJo's mummy when she was a little girl inspires JoJo and Gran Gran to take a walk and compare old photos of their neighbourhood to see how it has changed over time.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: A fair is being held in the park, and Sarah and Duck can't find their bench.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: It's Small's birthday. Tiny is planning to put on a special show for him, which involves using her scooter and calling on help from the froglets and the singing flowers. But her scooter has developed a squeak. She calls on Major to help her mend it, but when Small accidentally overhears them talking about a scooter, he thinks that must be what he is getting for his present and he's very pleased. But what he ends up with is not quite what he expected - and much more exciting.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa learn how some things are bigger and others are smaller.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle is taking a ride on the Ninky Nonk. Upsy Daisy then gets on, she and Igglepiggle wave to each other from their different carriages. The Ninky Nonk travels around the garden and soon everybody is on board.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A different story read by a special guest each night just before bedtime.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Held captive at Frank and Marie's during a snowstorm, Debra, Ray and Amy actually find themselves having a great time until Debra makes a nasty comment to Frank that totally upsets the good mood.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray learns that Troup Leader Peggy and her daughter have targeted his parents house as a spot to make a big sale for themselves during the Frontier Girls selling competition, it's all-out war.

programme.title: Frasier

She is determined to use hospital techniques in her kitchen and is constantly sealing everything into plastic bags. Dr Morris is as stubborn as she is proud. Can Gordon help?

programme.desc: Held captive at Frank and Marie's during a snowstorm, Debra, Ray and Amy actually find themselves having a great time until Debra makes a nasty comment to Frank that totally upsets the good mood.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray learns that Troup Leader Peggy and her daughter have targeted his parents house as a spot to make a big sale for themselves during the Frontier Girls selling competition, it's all-out war.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier encourages everyone to take a `bonus day' and do something special to celebrate the leap year.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier resorts to physical force in order to teach manners to a rather rude patron at the Cafe Nervosa.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier's antique scout has found for him an antique chess set, and can't wait to play with it. Niles declines, explaining that it will remind him too much of Maris as they used to play every Thursday night.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles has been asked to testify, on Court TV, against an old millionaire Harlow Safford. His son believes he is mentally unfit to run his own estate, and wants it handed over to him.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Pasadena, California to visit a Jamaican restaurant called Café Kingston. There, Gordon finds Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works as a radiotherapist in a clinic next door to the restaurant. She is determined to use hospital techniques in her kitchen and is constantly sealing everything into plastic bags. Dr Morris is as stubborn as she is proud. Can Gordon help?

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4's midday news summary.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire, Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole meet mangle collector Colin, who's trying to find a home for his late father's possessions, including a motorbike he won in a raffle!

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: Ted and Lesa embrace tent-living while renovating their French village. Penny hopes to sell her Italian borgo for a million euros. Steve and Julie's hamlet has a gruesome secret.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Lucy Porter in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Lisa from Bedfordshire find the perfect holiday home for her and her family in northern Tenerife, known as the Island of Eternal Spring and famous for its volcanic black-sand beaches.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Seán and Áine plan the Salty Shamrock pub's annual St Puppies Day show. Nick struggles to light his Brits-only BBQ. Is Laura's Olympic equestrian career over before it's begun?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château des Fouilloux, Sophie and Ray start work on a tower that's unfit for a princess. At Château Trois Cloches, Chris and Jolyon race to finish their long-awaited bathroom.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, the Rodriguezes search with Sara from Devon, who has decided to swap hospital beds for sun beds on the Spanish Costas after 34 years with the NHS. In Málaga, the Hamiltons search for a pooch-friendly pad with Joy and Bernd.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: When it comes to dating etiquette, Breeze the American bulldog cross leaves a lot to be desired when he meets 31-year-old Charlotte. Will she still fall for him though?

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: As the group learn the art of seduction in the penultimate phase, Jason lowers his guard, Ellen continues to flourish, and the number of ex-virgins is set to increase.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: As the retreat concludes, Bertie heads for the bedroom with Kat for the lesson of a lifetime. Katie and Callum get together. And has Marianne finally overcome her mistrust of men?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Will former mayor Tracey take the plunge with toilet roll salesman Stuart? And can burlesque dancer Double D and professional singer Lee come together and hit all the right notes?

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: On the American Pacific coast, a female fraudster and leading local pagan feigns serious illness to steal over $500,000 from her victims. And then the FBI gets involved.

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: Further Novichok casualties cause panic in the community. As the case becomes a murder investigation, how should the UK respond to such blatant Russian aggression.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to America in a bid to rescue failing restaurant owners from imminent disaster and provide them with a recipe for success. In this first episode, Gordon visits the Hot Potato Café in Fishtown, Philadelphia. With no profit made in its two years of trading and an influential restaurant reviewer dubbing them `Spuddy Hell', Gordon soon realises Hot Potato may have had its chips.

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: Sketch comedy queen Doon Mackichan joins Sandi in beautiful Donegal, where they discover a beachside architectural behemoth, and music and laughter in a lochside eco-resort.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn need help with the layout and decoration of their living room, while Kay plans her first ever craft fair.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn need help with the layout and decoration of their living room, while Kay plans her first ever craft fair.

programme.title: I Can't Get No Sleep!
programme.desc: Fancy a mash up after midnight? Only massive, ad free anthems to help with your insomnia! More beats, less sleep!

programme.title: Pumpin' Eurodance Bangers!
programme.desc: Melodic synths, pumping beats and shout out loud lyrics. Live your best life with some Eurodance NRG!

programme.title: Party Rock is in the House!
programme.desc: Bangin' party tunes for the ultimate dance karaoke session, if that's your thing. Clubland does it best!

programme.title: Endless Clubland Classics!
programme.desc: The sound that started it all! Join us for a selection of legendary Clubland classics. Expect high-energy beats, the biggest vocal anthems and feel good party vibe for all!

programme.title: A Billion Streams! The 2020s
programme.desc: Which 2020s tracks have smashed a billion streams worldwide? Think Tiësto, Guetta, Corry and a few heavy hitters from Dua!

programme.title: Judge Jules: 90s Ibiza Anthems
programme.desc: Ibiza in the 90s, what a time to be alive. Join Judge Jules and his hand picked Balearic breakdowns, pumping basslines and killer beats. Lets go back to the party island.

programme.title: Essential 90s: The Club Classics!
programme.desc: Need a fix of 90s club classics? Lock in for a marathon of memories as we take you back to the greatest decade of dance! The 90s!

programme.title: Robin's Rave Cave! Top 25

programme.title: Strictly Old Skool!
programme.desc: Let's go way back with some classic throwback hits, long forgotten beats and some good old-fashioned rave anthems!

programme.title: 40 Huge Floorfillers of the 2020s
programme.desc: 40 blistering tracks from 2020 to 2025. With Calvin, Guetta, Doja, Gaga and so many more awesome tunes!

programme.title: Future Classics! The 2020s
programme.desc: No excuses, stop what you are doing, turn it up and party hard with the biggest anthems and future classics of the twenty twenties!

programme.title: Tulisa's Back to the Old Skool
programme.desc: Tulisa selects 25 classics from the first decade of this century, including N-Dubz, Tinchy, DJ Pied Piper and Eric Prydz.

programme.title: Clubland UK No.1s! 1995-2015
programme.desc: 30 classics from the archives which all reached the summit of the official UK charts. From Outhere Brothers to Avicii via Vengaboys and DJ Casper.

programme.title: Endless Clubland Classics!
programme.desc: The sound that started it all! Join us for a selection of legendary Clubland classics. Expect high-energy beats, the biggest vocal anthems and feel good party vibe for all!

programme.desc: Remember these? Five hours of massive tunes and banging beats, that you just might have forgotten about.

programme.sub-title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out

programme.desc: Phoebe doesn't get on with her identical twin Ursula. Unfortunately, no one can tell them apart, which leads to a few cases of mistaken identity, not least for Joey who is quite taken with Ursula.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers

programme.title: Russell Howard: Wonderbox Live

programme.desc: Les and Ashley get reeled in by the World's Largest Fishing Lure, but Seth thinks it will be a tough sell; Les squares off with a hot headed hillbilly.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: After Ashley smells weed in the warehouse, the hunt is on to find out who is getting high at work. Will Les's big cash reward bring out the snitches or will Seth's plan to drug test their employees reveal the culprit?

programme.title: Salvage Kings
programme.desc: Ted and Justin travel to Market Village Mall in Markham, Ontario, where they are tasked with carefully unlocking mysterious vault doors while the demolition team begins tear down.

programme.title: Salvage Kings
programme.desc: At the site of the former Yugoslavian Consulate, Ted hopes to salvage invaluable secret relics of the Cold War. Back at the National, a buyer shows interest in a vintage WW II truck but Ted has to convince him the price is right.

programme.title: Salvage Kings
programme.desc: Ted and Justin salvage at a century-old warehouse that was once the mail order depot for one of North America's largest department stores. Ted promises Ryan big profits from the timber inside the massive structure, but things don't go as planned.

programme.title: Salvage Kings
programme.desc: While hunting for salvage in a 1870s farmhouse, Ted finds something rare hidden behind the walls. Elsewhere, the demolition team are put to the test.

programme.title: Salvage Kings
programme.desc: The demolition and salvage team at Priestly Demolition Inc is in the business of tearing down buildings, but first, they give their salvage crew the chance to go inside and recover historic treasures left behind.

programme.title: Salvage Kings
programme.desc: The demo and salvage teams head to picturesque Manitoulin Island hoping to uncover architectural salvage and long-forgotten treasures.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques dealers Debbie Serpell and Stewart Hofgartner visit Gloucester, where a music stand, a sword and a brooch are among the items brought in to be valued by members of the public.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antique dealers Ian Towning, Cheryl Hakeney, James Layte and Tony Geering visit Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, where they drive hard bargains for their favourite items, and a set of family photographs causes a stir.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Mechanics Will and Gus go in search of iconic British sports cars for under £1000. They find a classic MG F to refurbish, but the car has many minor problems.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The team knows that the BMW Mini is a solid and popular car. They decide to buy one for six hundred pounds on eBay. But cheap turns out to be expensive when problems come to light.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will repair a famous small car: a 1989 Citroën 2CV, a model produced from 1948 to 1990.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: This once £35,000 car has been given up on. The previous owner can't get the roof to work or the car to run, so they are selling it.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers

programme.title: Flipping Bangers

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
